part.part_title: Special Populations and Life Course Approach

learning_objectives.text: Define key maternal and child health indicators including maternal mortality ratio, infant mortality rate, and under-5 mortality rate
Explain the continuum of care approach for maternal, newborn, and child health across the life course
Describe the main causes of maternal and child mortality globally and their distribution by region and income level
Calculate and interpret maternal and child health indicators using global health data sources
Design evidence-based maternal and child health interventions appropriate for different health system contexts
Analyze the social determinants contributing to maternal and child health disparities across different populations
Compare the effectiveness of community-based versus facility-based maternal and child health interventions
Evaluate the impact of integrated maternal and child health programs on health outcomes and health system performance
Develop a comprehensive maternal and child health strategy that addresses multiple levels of the health system

case_studies.title: Rwanda's Maternal Mortality Reduction: Community Health Cooperatives and Performance-Based Financing
case_studies.focus: Maternal mortality reduction through health system strengthening
case_studies.outcomes: MMR reduced from 1,071 per 100,000 in 2000 to 210 per 100,000 in 2017

case_studies.title: Brazil's Criança Feliz Program: Integrated Early Childhood Development
case_studies.focus: Child survival and development through home visiting
key_interventions: Home visiting program for vulnerable families
Integration with Bolsa Família conditional cash transfer
case_studies.outcomes: Improved vaccination coverage, reduced child mortality, enhanced child development outcomes

case_studies.title: India's RMNCH+A Strategy: Scaling Reproductive, Maternal, Newborn, Child Health and Adolescent Programs
case_studies.focus: Comprehensive life course approach to MCH
case_studies.outcomes: MMR decreased from 556 in 1990 to 103 per 100,000 in 2017-19; U5MR from 126 to 35 per 1,000 live births

case_studies.title: Ghana's Community-based Health Planning and Services (CHPS): Mobile Technology for Pregnancy Monitoring
case_studies.focus: Innovation in maternal health service delivery through mobile health
key_interventions: Mobile health platform for pregnancy tracking
case_studies.outcomes: Increased antenatal care utilization, improved birth preparedness, reduced maternal and neonatal complications

high_income: examples for comparison and best practices

items.question: Which of the following best defines the maternal mortality ratio (MMR)?
options: Number of maternal deaths per 1,000 live births
Number of maternal deaths per 100,000 live births
Number of maternal deaths per 1,000 women of reproductive age
Number of maternal deaths per 100,000 women of reproductive age
items.correct: Number of maternal deaths per 100,000 live births
items.explanation: MMR is defined as the number of maternal deaths during pregnancy, childbirth, or within 42 days of termination of pregnancy per 100,000 live births in a given time period.

items.question: According to WHO recommendations, what percentage of births should be attended by skilled birth attendants to significantly reduce maternal mortality?
items.explanation: WHO recommends that at least 90% of births should be attended by skilled birth attendants to achieve significant reductions in maternal and neonatal mortality.

items.question: Which intervention has been shown to be most effective in preventing neonatal deaths in low-resource settings?
options: Kangaroo mother care for preterm infants
Early initiation of breastfeeding and thermal care
items.explanation: Multiple interventions including early breastfeeding, thermal care, and kangaroo mother care work synergistically to prevent neonatal deaths.

items.question: Explain the 'three delays' model in maternal mortality and provide an example intervention for each delay.
key_points: Delay in deciding to seek care (education, empowerment)
Delay in reaching appropriate care (transport, infrastructure)
Delay in receiving quality care (skilled providers, equipment)

items.question: Describe the components of the continuum of care for maternal, newborn, and child health.
key_points: Newborn care (immediate, first week, first month)
Adolescent health and reproductive health preparation
